title: Why tag cardinality dominates cost
description: Each unique tag-value combination is a separate series with its own index entry and memory; cardinality grows multiplicatively across tags.
body: Use when designing the schema or diagnosing memory pressure. Series count = product of distinct values across all tags; adding a high-cardinality tag (user id) multiplies total series and the in-memory index. Do not place identifiers or unbounded strings in tags/labels — keep them in fields, which are not indexed per-value. Actions: enumerate tag value ranges and estimate the product before deploying. Validation signals include series cardinality within the engine's tested limit. Risk boundary: cardinality blowup degrades the entire instance, not a single query.
